ops.title: cookiesync Phase 4: routed denial now VerdictDenied (was Unavailable)
ops.body:
Phase 4 cutover onto synckit consent/ + authkit (commit 5769cb0 on branch authkit-migration).

**Denial-classification choice (adopted the more-correct one):** cookiesync's old `routedConsent` wrapped a routed peer denial in `*AuthRequired`, which `Classify` mapped to `VerdictUnavailable`. The generic `consent.Router.Route` returns a terminal `*consent.Denied` for a routed decline, which `consent.Classify` (delegated to by cookiesync's `auth.Classify`) maps to `VerdictDenied`. I adopted the Denied classification (did NOT wrap at the broker). Control flow is unchanged — both are terminal, no failover to other peers — only the classification differs. User-visible: a routed decline now renders "consent declined" instead of "run cookiesync auth". The approver-side path (`handleRequestConsent` local decline -> cookie.ConsentError -> Denied) was already Denied and is unaffected.

**Other behavior deltas:** prompt reason env var COOKIESYNC_TOUCHID_REASON -> AUTHKIT_REASON; helper cask cookiesync-keyhelper -> authkit (via authkit.RequireHelper Caskroom glob; AUTHKIT_HELPER override); IsUnknownSubcommand skew fallback removed (authkit always supports vault-batch-retrieve, so exit-2 now always means no-biometry -> bare Keychain read); Router probe parse-error wording "parse whoami" -> "parse presence". Keychain access group carried by authkit -> existing `cookiesync.vault.<browser>` items stay readable, no re-enrollment.

**Broker.Router is now an exported field** (narrow test seam, replacing the old exported `Nonce` field) so the sibling daemon package's tests can pin the routed-consent nonce. synckit consent imported as alias `consentkit` in auth/daemon to avoid clashing with the `consent cookie.Consent` field.</body>
<parameter name="commits">["5769cb0"]
